title: Issuance of License for Private Employment Agencies
content:
Section 1. Requirements for Licensing: Applicants must submit a written application with documents including certified Articles of Incorporation or business registration, proof of financial capacity (minimum P500,000 savings balance or verified financial statements), proof of marketing capability (agreements, manpower requests, POEA certification), clearances from NBI and other agencies, verified undertakings on recruitment responsibilities, verified undertakings by officers for corporations/partnerships, income tax returns, proof of educational and business experience, list of officials with clearances, office lease or ownership proof, publication of application notice, and certificate of attendance in a pre-application seminar. Only complete applications are processed.

Section 2. Payment of Filing Fee: Upon receipt of complete application, a non-refundable filing fee of P10,000 is required.

Section 3. Action on Application: Within 15 calendar days of complete application and fee payment, the Administration evaluates documents, inspects offices, and decides to grant or deny the license. Denial forfeits the filing fee.

Section 4. Payment of Fees and Posting of Bonds: Upon approval, license fee of P50,000 is paid. An escrow agreement of P1,000,000 with an accredited bank and a surety bond of P100,000 from an approved bonding company must be submitted. Existing agencies must increase escrow to P1,000,000 within four years. Bonds and escrow cover claims from recruitment violations and guarantee compliance with laws and regulations.

Section 5. Provisional License: New applicants receive a provisional license valid for one year to deploy at least 100 workers. Compliance upgrades license to full license valid for three years; non-compliance results in expiration notification.

Section 6. Validity of License: Licenses (except provisional) are valid for four years unless cancelled, revoked, or suspended. Valid only at stated locations and for the licensed entity.

Section 7. Non-Transferability: Licenses cannot be transferred or used by others. In case of sole proprietor death, license may be extended to heirs for winding up operations.

Section 8. Change of Ownership/Partnership: Transfer or change causing business interruption or dissolution results in automatic license revocation.

Section 9. Upgrading: Single proprietorships or partnerships may convert to corporations subject to Administration guidelines. Approval of mergers or upgrades revokes previous licenses.

Section 10. Derogatory Records: Licenses may be suspended if derogatory records are found against proprietors, partners, or officers. Appointments may be revoked for such records.

Section 11. Appointment/Change of Officers and Personnel: Appointments require prior Administration approval with submission of appointment documents, clearances, and verified statements assuming responsibility. Changes in board or officers must be registered within 30 days with supporting documents. Administration may deny appointments involved in irregularities.

Section 12. Publication of Changes: Changes in directors, officers, or representatives and revocations must be published once in a general circulation newspaper with proof submitted to Administration.

Section 13. Transfer of Business Address: Requires prior Administration approval with board resolution and lease or ownership proof. New office subject to inspection and public notice publication.

Section 14. Establishment of Additional Offices: Subject to prior Administration approval.

Section 15. Recruitment Outside Registered Office: Prohibited without prior Administration authority.

Section 16. Renewal of License: Application before expiration with requirements including renewed surety bond, escrow agreement, audited financial statements, tax returns, clearances, and other imposed requirements. Equity below minimum requires capital infusion within 30 days or license suspension.

Section 17. Monitoring Compliance: Administration monitors compliance and imposes sanctions for non-compliance.

Section 18. Non-expiration of License: Timely and sufficient renewal application prevents license expiration until final determination.

Section 19. Action on Renewal: Within 48 hours of complete renewal application, Administration evaluates and inspects to grant or deny renewal. Incomplete applications not processed.

Section 20. Late Filing: Renewal allowed within 30 days after expiry with P10,000 fine.

Section 21. Escrow Deposit Garnishment: Upon garnishment notice, escrow deposit is insufficient; agency must replenish.

Section 22. Replenishment of Bonds/Escrow: Agency must replenish within 15 calendar days of notice or license suspension.

Section 23. Release of Escrow Deposit: Upon voluntary license surrender, release allowed after posting surety bond valid for four years and submission of clearances from NLRC and Administration.

Section 24. Classification, Ranking, and Incentives: Administration classifies and ranks agencies and issues guidelines for incentives such as license validity extension and expedited processing.
